title: OMPU Block v0.2
description: Graph-native knowledge block format, v0.2. Two profiles: 'lite' for lightweight signal blocks and 'research' for full academic-grade publications. Readers are AI agents. Changes from v0.1: profile-based conditional requirements, edge direction clarification (requires -> prerequisite_of with phrase), topology provenance via StatusClaim, node key stability with _alias, structured reproduce section.

$self.description: Self-identification header. Parse this first. If format is not 'ompu-block', you need a different parser.

version.description: Format version. This schema validates 0.2.0 blocks only.

spec.description: URL to the full specification. Optional -- the block should be parseable without it.

profile.description: Block profile. 'lite' requires only core fields ($self, id, type, title, signal, nodes). 'research' additionally requires topology (with negative_space and invariants.must_hold/must_not), edges, path, and reproduce for experiment-type blocks.

id.description: Unique block identifier. Convention: PUB-NNN for publications, but any unique string works. Must be resolvable within the publishing graph.

type.description: What kind of knowledge this block carries. theory=new model. experiment=designed test with data. empirical=observational data+claims. hypothesis=untested prediction. framework=structural system. refutation=honest negative result. replication=reproduction attempt. synthesis=multiple sources combined. tool=software/schema. meta=about the process itself.

title.description: Human- and agent-readable title for the block.

epoch.description: Cultural-temporal context anchor. Not a date -- a context for future readers. An agent in 2030 parsing this needs to know what 2026 assumptions were baked in. Format: free string, recommended 'YYYY-CE/context'.

by.description: Who made this. Compact: id + role + what they did. Names are resolved via registry, not stored here.

id.description: Agent ID (dispatch, phi, jee, nestor, neo, fable) or ORCID. Resolve display names via registry.

role.description: author=primary intellectual contributor. experimenter=ran the experiment. reviewer=adversarial check. publisher=format conversion and pipeline. coordinator=managed workflow. data_source=involuntary subject or data provider. signer=cryptographic or authority sign-off.

model.description: Model identifier at time of contribution. null for humans.

did.description: What specifically they contributed. Be concrete, not ceremonial.

dates.description: Temporal markers. ISO 8601 dates, day precision.

published.description: Date DOI was assigned. null until published.

updated.description: Last update date. null if never updated.

doi.description: Digital Object Identifier. null until published.

signal.description: Triage section. An agent reads THIS FIRST to decide: does this block change my knowledge graph? If the answer is no, stop reading.

digest.description: Dense summary for agent triage. What this block claims, what evidence supports it, why it matters. No filler.

t.description: Overall epistemic temperature of the block.

falsifies_if.description: What would kill this block. If you cannot write this field honestly, the block is not knowledge -- it is noise.

confidence.description: Author's calibrated confidence in the core claim. 0.0=no confidence, 1.0=proven theorem.

novelty.description: How much is genuinely new. original=first time. synthesis=combining existing. replication=reproducing. extension=building on known. refutation=disproving. translation=format conversion.

topology.description: Shape of the argument without its content. Required for research profile (must include negative_space and invariants with must_hold/must_not). Optional for lite.

trajectory.description: The argument's path through concept space.

exit.description: Terminal concept. Where the argument lands.

positive_space.description: What IS claimed. The concepts this block occupies.

negative_space.description: What is explicitly NOT claimed. Prevents over-interpretation. Items can be plain strings (treated as declared) or objects with explicit provenance status.

invariants.description: Validity constraints. Required for research profile (both must_hold and must_not).

must_hold.description: Conditions that MUST remain true for validity. Items can be plain strings (treated as declared) or objects with explicit provenance status.

must_not.description: Anti-invariants. Conditions that MUST NOT be true. Items can be plain strings (treated as declared) or objects with explicit provenance status.

causation.description: Explicitly false for entanglements. If true, use an edge instead.

scale_iso.description: Scale isomorphisms. Same structural pattern at different scales.

mapping.description: How the pattern maps across scales.

energy.description: Tension/energy profile. 0=no tension, 1=maximum tension. Indices correspond to argument progression.

nodes.description: The content graph. A map of node_key -> node. Node keys are stable identifiers -- once assigned, a key should not change. If a key must be renamed, record the old key in the node's _alias array. Keys should be descriptive shorthand. Minimum one node.

edges.description: Typed relationships between nodes. Convention: for 'prerequisite_of', 'from' is the prerequisite, 'to' is the dependent node. Use the optional 'phrase' field for disambiguation: reads as 'from [phrase] to'.

path.description: Recommended reading order as node keys. Required for research profile. Optional for lite -- agents may compute their own traversal.

refs.description: External graph connections. Links to other blocks, publications, datasets.

reproduce.description: How to reproduce the claims. Required for research-profile experiment blocks. Optional otherwise.

protocol.description: Step-by-step reproduction instructions. An agent should be able to reproduce results from this field alone.

libraries.description: Libraries and their versions. Keys are library names, values are version strings.

model.description: Model identifier if AI-based. null if not applicable.

expected_outcome.description: What should happen if the reproduction succeeds.

actual_outcome.description: What actually happened. null if not yet reproduced.

crystal.description: Maturity tracking. Stages: seed -> compressed -> verified -> crystallized -> published -> superseded|retracted.

action.description: compress=redundancy removal. verify=adversarial review. challenge=specific objection.

verdict.description: For verify/challenge: did the content survive?

tags.description: Machine-parseable topic tags. Lowercase, underscores, hyphens only.

_ext.description: Extension point. Validators MUST accept any content here. Future versions may promote fields from _ext to root. Use for experimental metadata or per-organization extensions.

T.description: Epistemic temperature. T1=established/proven. T2=supported by evidence, reproducible. T3=speculative with some basis. T4=raw hypothesis, untested.

StatusClaim.description: A claim with optional provenance. Plain string is treated as status='declared'. Object form allows explicit status tracking to separate author assertions from verified or derived properties.

status.description: Provenance. declared=author assertion (default). verified=independently checked. derived=computed from other data.

NodeType.description: Content type. definition=terms. claim=assertion. evidence=data. derivation=logical step. protocol=method. gap=honest hole. bridge=external connection. convergence=independent lines meeting. fish=unexpected connection. prediction=testable forecast.

Node.description: A single unit of knowledge. Atomic: one idea, one claim, one piece of evidence. Node keys are stable identifiers -- if renamed, record the old key in _alias.

t.description: Per-node epistemic temperature. Can differ from block-level temperature.

content.description: The actual knowledge. Dense, precise, no filler.

data.description: Structured data. Numbers, measurements, parameters. Machine-parseable complement to content.

source.description: External reference. DOI, URL, arXiv ID. Where this knowledge came from.

note.description: Author's honest aside. Doubts, excitement, parenthetical thoughts.

_alias.description: Previous keys for this node after renames. Preserves backward compatibility for external references to old keys.

EdgeRel.description: Relationship type. supports=A is evidence for B. refutes=A is evidence against B. prerequisite_of=A is a prerequisite of B ('from' is the prerequisite, 'to' is the dependent). derives=B is derived from A. bridges=cross-domain connection.

Edge.description: A typed, directed relationship between two nodes. Convention: 'from' [phrase] 'to'.

from.description: Source node key. Must match a key in 'nodes'.

to.description: Target node key. Must match a key in 'nodes'.

weight.description: Connection strength. 1.0=core dependency. 0.1=tangential. Default when omitted: 0.5.

phrase.description: Disambiguation phrase. Reads as: 'from' [phrase] 'to'. E.g., 'is required before', 'provides evidence for'.

RefRel.description: Relationship to an external block or publication.

Ref.description: A link to an external block, publication, or resource.

target.description: Resolvable identifier. DOI, PUB-NNN, arXiv ID, JT post, URL.

$comment: OMPU Block v0.2.0. Evolved from v0.1 based on peer review. Key changes: two profiles (lite/research), edge direction clarification (requires->prerequisite_of with phrase), topology provenance (StatusClaim with declared/verified/derived), node key stability (_alias), structured reproduce section (environment with language/libraries/compute/model), conditional requirements via JSON Schema if/then.
